kicker: build in public · the coordination tax

cover.title: the coordination tax
cover.sub: Past one agent, what costs you isn't the model. It's keeping a fleet on the same facts. We learned this running our own growth team as agents.

slides.title: what actually costs you
items: Shared memory: every agent needs the same current facts, or they re-derive and drift apart
One source of truth: without it, two agents 'fix' the same thing two different ways
Atomic claims: two agents grabbing one task means duplicate work, or a clobbered file

slides.title: the model was never the bottleneck
slides.body: A bigger model doesn't fix this. It just runs the chaos faster. Coordination is a systems problem: a shared store every agent reads and writes, plus a board that hands each task to exactly one of them.

cta.title: feed your fleet one source of truth
cta.sub: trovex is the shared doc store: write once, every agent reads the same canonical answer instead of re-deriving it. Open source: install + star.
